hvac_types.FC4: 4-pipe fan coil unit (also 2-pipe cold water only with electric heat)
hvac_types.FW2: 2-pipe fan coil with backup electric heat (requires pipe temp sensor)
hvac_types.FC2: 2-pipe fan coil without backup electric heat (requires pipe temp sensor)
hvac_types.HbW: Heat Pump B with W2 assist
hvac_types.HpB: Heat Pump B with W2 replace
hvac_types.HoW: Heat Pump O with W2 assist
hvac_types.HpO: Heat Pump O with W2 replace

initialization_mode: Ensure thermostat is mounted and connected to the Smart Wall Plate before starting.
When properly connected, thermostat enters INITIALIZATION MODE and displays 'rId'.
If not connected, unit displays 'SWp' alert until Smart Wall Plate is connected.
set_room_id: Once 'rId' is displayed press MODE button. Default Room ID (65535) scrolls across screen.
The 5-digit Room ID is entered in three parts: highest digit, middle two digits, lowest two digits.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to set the highest digit (range 0-6) then press the FAN button to confirm.
Set the middle 2 digits (range 0-99) and press FAN button to confirm.
Set the lowest 2 digits (range 0-99) and press MODE button to confirm.
The new Room ID scrolls across the display and the unit beeps when stored to memory.
Press DOWN arrow to display HVAC menu.
set_hvac_type: Once HVAC menu is displayed press MODE button.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to select the correct HVAC type from the hvac_types list.
Press FAN button to accept. Unit beeps to confirm.
Press DOWN arrow to display Fan Speed menu.
set_fan_speed: Use UP/DOWN arrows to select fan speed: 00, Lo, LH, or LMh.
NOTE: For heat pump models using reversing valve, medium fan speed is not available.
Press FAN button to execute. Unit beeps to confirm.
Press the temp unit button to exit INITIALIZATION MODE.
'STr' is displayed confirming setup values stored in memory.
Unit reboots and begins operating HVAC as configured.
entering_service_mode: Press and hold the temp unit button.
While holding, press and release MODE, then press and release FAN.
Release the temp unit button. Thermostat is now in Service Mode.
resetting_room_id_in_service_mode: NOTE: Only follow this if Room ID needs to change after Initialization Mode.
In service mode navigate to 'rId' parameter and press MODE.
Default Room ID (65535) scrolls across screen.
Set highest digit and press FAN. Set middle 2 digits and press FAN. Set lowest 2 digits and press MODE.
New Room ID scrolls across screen to confirm.
setting_pan_id_and_rf_channel: INNCOM Deep Mesh Network uses PAN ID and RF Channel to group rooms per Edge Router.
Assign PAN ID and RF Channel as per Edge Router configuration in EngINN.
For standalone property or model room: set PAN ID to 1 and RF Channel to 26.
In service mode navigate to 'PAn' (PAN ID) and press MODE.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to set the PAN ID and press FAN to confirm.
Navigate to RF Channel parameter, press MODE, set correct RF Channel, press FAN to confirm.
enginn_configuration: Requires PC/laptop, dongle PC-503, and Inncom proprietary tool EngINN.
In EngINN Deployment Data window populate the Room ID.
Verify RF Channel, PAN ID, and Room ID in EngINN match the installed E7.
Assign the same RF Channel for PC503 in EngINN.
In EngINN go to Deployment Data, Room Type, select the E7 thermostat and click BIND.
Press Ping button to confirm device is online.
Click Write Configuration to upload. Task Manager window shows upload status.
change_e7_address_multi_hvac_suite: In service mode navigate to E7 parameter and press MODE.
Use UP/DOWN arrows to set the E7 address and press FAN to confirm.
Press temp unit button to exit service mode.
Click Write Configuration in EngINN to upload the new address.
binding_s541_rfd_occupancy_sensor: In EngINN go to Deployment Data, Room Type, select the S541.RFD device and click BIND.
Press Send button to transmit the bind message.
Press the blue S1 switch on the S541.RF to accept the bind.
Success: LED flashes 3 times and EngINN beeps 3 times and displays Device is online.
binding_k594_rf_door_sensor: In EngINN go to Deployment Data, Room Type, select the K594.RF device and click BIND.
Press Send button to transmit the bind message.
Press the recessed Bind button next to the status LED on the K594.RF using a paper clip.
Success: LED flashes 3 times and EngINN beeps 3 times and displays Device is online.
checking_functionality: HVAC: Test all available fan speeds, cooling and heating operations.
Balcony/Window Switch: In service mode navigate to 'bln' (Balcony) and press MODE.
Thermostat displays and beeps if balcony is open.
When closed thermostat counts open/close events. Allow count to reach at least 3 for valid test.
Press temp unit button 3 times to return to normal operation.

onity_trillium_lock_integration.topic: E7 to Onity Trillium 1.0 / Advance Trillium 1.0 Lock - How to Join/Pair Procedure
onity_trillium_lock_integration.source_document: AN396 E7 Onity Trillium 1.0 Lock Reference Guide, Version 1.0
onity_trillium_lock_integration.overview: Onity Trillium 1.0 locks use 1-Way RMS Communication with Onity's DirectKey Encryption Protocol. Each lock has a property-specific encryption key retrieved from Onity's CoreAPI DirectKey Server. All encrypted BLE messages are initiated FROM the lock - the E7 never initiates communication toward the lock, but must know the encryption key and lock serial# to decrypt messages sent from the lock. INNTool/EngINN is required to obtain the key/serial# from Onity and write them to the E7.

required_versions.E7_firmware: 1.2.51.0 (special Onity Trillium build, NOT the generic INNTool shelf software)

hardware_notes.supported_thermostats: As of 5/2026, only D1-528 thermostat, DX47 HVAC controller, and E7 thermostat support Trillium 1.0 locks
hardware_notes.max_mounting_distance_E7_to_lock: 30 ft (9 meters) for both Trillium 1.0 and Advance Trillium 1.0
distance_issue_symptoms: Room occupancy state not correct or not changing (E7 never receives Guest/Staff card indication)
Welcome lighting not turning on, or significant delay
If unacceptable, install wireless S541.RF or wired door switch on E7 low voltage input

prerequisites.network: PC/laptop running INNTool/EngINN must have an active internet connection so EngINN can reach Onity's CoreAPI DirectKey Server

step_0_coordination_before_installation.description: Required coordination between Inncom project team and Onity before any pairing can occur
steps: Decide which rooms will have E7 + Trillium 1.0 lock, and whether each lock is Trillium 1.0 or Advance Trillium 1.0
Onity creates the project in OnPortal and adds the Locks, ensuring Lock ID's match the Room#'s used in the INNTool project
Inncom builds an INNTool project with E7 thermostats and Onity Trillium 1.0/Advance Trillium 1.0 locks defined, ensuring the list of rooms (and E7 Room ID) matches what Onity defined in OnPortal

For an EXISTING installation where locks are already installed but never BLE-paired: Onity must supply a list of Lock Room#'s/ID's and lock type; Inncom loads the existing INNProj into INNTool and creates new Room type(s) with the Trillium locks defined, then loads the updated INNProj into EngINN

An authorized member of Inncom's project team (using a shared/restricted mailbox) emails Onity Tier 3 support at mobilecredentials@honeywell.com requesting the OwnerID, API certificate + certificate password, and API Username & Password for the property. Email format: Subject = 'Directkey API Certificate for "Property name"'; Body = 'Hi Tier 3, Please send the OwnerID, API certificate and certificate password, API Username & Password for property "Property name and address"'

step_1_create_inntool_project.description: Create/verify the INNTool project defines the E7 with the correct Onity Trillium lock settings (skip if already created)
steps: Open INNTool and click 'Create New Project'
Define Project Code, Sales Representative, Property Name, Networking, and other standard settings
Click 'Add New Room Type' and step through the wizard; on the Thermostat step select 'e7 Series - e7 Thermostat with Built In Relays' as the thermostat
On the EMS Options page, select 'Electronic Door Locks (CELS) and Motion Sensor', then choose CELS Type = 'Onity Trillium 1.0' or 'Onity Trillium 1.0 Advance'
A new dropdown appears with two choices: 'Only Motion Sensor, No Door Switch' (default) or 'Door Switch'.

If 'Only Motion Sensor, No Door Switch' is chosen: Registry 9:13:8 bits KCDL(4) and DAC(3) get SET so the E7 automatically sends 0x000281 door open after a valid card use, then 0x000280 door close after the 9:13:9 AutoDoorCloseTimeout (default 8 sec) elapses.

If 'Door Switch' is chosen: a new prompt 'What type of Door Switch do you require?' appears (Wired or Wireless/S541.RF); this adds the switch/S541.RF to the BOM and Registry 9:13:8 bits KCDL(4) and DAC(3) get CLEARED so the E7 does NOT auto-send 0x000281/0x000280 - the wired switch or S541.RF reports door position instead (faster, no lock-reporting delay)
Finish the New Project wizard to create the Room Type containing the E7 and the selected Trillium lock configuration

step_2_load_project_into_engInn.description: Load the project into EngINN and verify room numbering matches Onity's lock database
steps: Load the created project .Innproj file into EngINN; add any required rooms if not already added in INNTool

CRITICAL: Confirm with Onity the list of Lock room#'s and make sure the rooms added to EngINN EXACTLY match the lock room#'s (e.g., Room 205 must exist in OnPortal, must be set into the physical lock via PPCom programmer, and the E7 in that room must be set to Room ID 205)

step_3_onity_authentication_manager_get_.description: Use EngINN's Onity Authentication Manager to download the property-specific DirectKey encryption key and the lock serial# database from Onity's CoreAPI DirectKey Server
steps: In EngINN, click the 'Onity Authentication' button (cloud icon on the main toolbar) to open the Onity Authentication Manager window
Ensure the PC running EngINN has an active internet connection
Enter the 5 required fields obtained from Onity Tier 3 support: Onity Server, Username, Password, Owner ID, Certificate Password, plus Browse to select the Certificate file
Click 'Get Onity Details'

On success, a 'Key Fetched successfully!' dialog appears; EngINN internally stores the site-specific encryption key and downloads/stores the lock serial# for every room (the key itself is never displayed/exported)
On failure (e.g., 'Invalid Certificate or its Password'), verify all fields were entered correctly; if it persists, contact Onity Tier 3 support (mobilecredentials@honeywell.com) to verify the values
IMPORTANT: If EngINN is closed/restarted, this authentication step must be repeated - the key is not persisted between EngINN sessions and the 6 fields are not stored
Note: Before this step succeeds, the 'Write Key' button for every room's E7 is disabled

step_4_write_key_and_config_per_room.description: For EACH room, go to the E7 in EngINN's Deployment Data tree and perform these sub-steps in order

steps.detail: Select the E7 for the room, click 'Write Key'. EngINN first sends a Read Device Type command to verify the device is actually an E7 and is communicating, then writes the Onity DirectKey encryption key to the E7. Success message: 'Upload Onity Secure Key completed.' If a PC503 connection error appears, verify the PC503 USB connection and RF Channel match the E7, then retry.

In EngINN's Deployment Data section, click on the Room (not the E7 device) to open Room Summary/Room Details, and confirm the 'Onity Serial Number' field is populated with the lock's serial# (this should match the serial# shown on the lock in Onity's DKT app).

steps.step: c. Write Configurations OR Write Onity CELS
steps.detail: Click either 'Write Configurations' (writes ALL CBL32 Registry values from the INNproj, including the Onity-specific ones: 9:1 Occupancy, 9:13 CELS, 193:12 BLE/lock serial# - use this for most new E7-Trillium installs), or 'Write Onity CELS Config' (writes ONLY the Onity-specific registries 9:1, 9:13, 193:12 - originally added for Inncom Direct D1-528/DX47 support where HVAC/Fan settings are already configured locally).

step_5_test_pairing_and_lock_communicati.description: Verify the E7 and lock are BLE paired and reporting correctly
steps: Confirm the BLE icon appears on the E7 display (display must be in active/non-dormant mode - press a button to wake it; also requires Registry 9:21:2 UserInteractiveModeDisplayMask bit 6 BLE to be set, which EngINN sets automatically for Trillium-configured E7s)

From the E7 service menu, use the Pn6 (Ping) parameter set to address 71 (NOT any other address) and press FAN to start; if pings succeed the E7 beeps and increments a counter every ~1 second when close to the lock (in testing at the 30 ft max distance, 50-60% of pings got replies)
Use a valid Guest or Staff card to unlock the door (optionally first place the E7 into Door Test mode - see Appendix B in the source document)
If KCDL/DAC bits are set (no external door switch): confirm you see 0x00075 CELS Card Operation followed by 0x000281 door open, then the delayed 0x000280 door close after the AutoDoorCloseTimeout

If KCDL/DAC bits are cleared (external S541.RF or wired switch installed): confirm the S541.RF/wired switch reports 0x000281 door open / 0x000280 door close directly, and the 0x00075 CELS Card Operation is reported separately by the lock
In IC3 Room View, confirm the 'Door Open' status updates correctly
Engage/disengage the lock's Deadbolt and confirm the CELS Status section on IC3 Room View shows 'DeadBolt' toggling correctly

Test Low Battery reporting: either install drained batteries in the lock, or temporarily raise Registry 9:13:0:12 (CELS_Cfg_Low_Batt_Thresh) to 255 to force a low-battery condition, confirm 'Low Lock Battery' appears in IC3's CELS Status section, then set 9:13:0:12 back to the default 14 (0x0E)
Verify HVAC and Fan speed control works bidirectionally between the E7 and IC3
Run the E7 WAN test to confirm connectivity to the Edge Router and IWAN programs

possible_causes: Lock's 4 AA batteries are dead or low
Lock was not set up/configured correctly by Onity to communicate BLE with the E7 (verify in Onity DKT that the lock room# and serial# are correct and the lock shows Online)
The E7 and lock were never actually BLE paired
E7 CBL32 Registry settings (9:1:0:13, 9:13:0:8/9/11/12, 193:12) are not configured correctly for the specific lock type/door-switch combination - see 'registry_reference_table' below

Registry 193:12 (BLE_Setup / lock serial#) is missing or was never written - EngINN downloads the serial# list on 'Get Onity Details' but does NOT automatically push it to the E7's Registry 193:12 until 'Write Configurations' or 'Write Onity CELS Config' is clicked
E7 and lock are physically farther apart than the 30 ft recommended maximum distance

registry_reference_table.description: Required CBL32 Registry values in the E7 based on lock type and whether an external door switch/S541.RF is used. Normally set automatically by EngINN based on INNTool selections.

configurations.config_name: Trillium 1.0 - No Door Switch or S541.RF
Trillium 1.0 - With door switch or S541.RF
Advance Trillium - No Door Switch or S541.RF
Advance Trillium - With door switch or S541.RF

manual_dialog_commands_optional_advanced.description: For advanced troubleshooting only - manual read/write commands if EngINN/GUI is unavailable. Not part of normal pairing workflow.
manual_dialog_commands_optional_advanced.read_193_12_lock_serial: 0018CC10C000F (result bytes must be BYTE-REVERSED then converted hex-to-decimal to get the actual serial#)
manual_dialog_commands_optional_advanced.write_193_12_lock_serial: 0018DC10C000FXXYYZZWW (XXYYZZWW = lock serial# in hex, BYTE-REVERSED order)

replacing_the_E7: Ensure replacement E7 is loaded with firmware v1.2.50 or later
Power down, disconnect old E7 from SmartWallPlate (SWP) - remove 2 screws, unplug 10-pin H1 harness and 8-pin H2 connector
Install replacement E7, reconnect 8-pin low voltage harness and 10-pin H1 harness, re-apply power
New E7 reads stored Registry configs from the SWP automatically (including 9:13 CELS and 193:12 BLE/serial#), but the encryption KEY is not restored this way
Re-run Step 3 (Onity Authentication Manager 'Get Onity Details') in EngINN, then click 'Write Key' for the room to push the encryption key into the new E7

If the SWP correctly restored all other registries, 'Write Configurations'/'Write Onity CELS Config' should not be needed - but test thoroughly (WAN test, Door test, guest/staff card test, deadbolt test)
replacing_the_lock: Onity technicians remove the old lock and install/configure the new lock using Onity DKT, OnPortal, and the PPCom programmer (updates the lock serial# tied to the Lock Room# in Onity's CoreAPI DirectKey Server)
The new lock has a different serial# than what is stored in the E7's Registry 193:12
Re-run Step 3 (Onity Authentication Manager 'Get Onity Details') in EngINN - this re-downloads the updated serial# list including the new lock's serial#
Click 'Write Key' (E7's key does not need to change, but this re-verifies E7 communication)
Click 'Write Onity CELS Config' to push the new lock serial# into Registry 193:12 of the existing E7 (Write Configurations is not required since HVAC/Fan settings on the E7 haven't changed)
Test thoroughly: WAN test, Door test, guest/staff card test, deadbolt set/clear test on IC3

onity_serene_lock_binding.title: Onity Serene Lock and E7 Binding/Pairing Procedure
onity_serene_lock_binding.source_doc: AN390 Onity Serene RF Lock Reference Guide, Version 4.0
onity_serene_lock_binding.applies_to: E7 thermostat configured as Onity Serene lock proxy only. Onity Trilium lock is NOT supported as of 7/2025.
prerequisites: E7 loaded with E7_Bundle_Shelf_Field_v1.2.48.0 or E7_Bundle_Spectre_Field_v1.2.48.0 or later.
E7 assigned its Room ID, PAN ID, and RF Channel.
E7 configured as an Onity lock proxy (Registry 193:9:1 IEEE OUI set to Onity IEEE OUI 00:17:55) via INNTool/EngINN, I/O Map 64, or dialog commands.
Onity has already installed, initialized, and configured the lock with its room number, using 'Initialize as Edge Device' option in OnPortal.
OnPortal PC has a BLE GigaDongle installed and working; OnPortal is running and logged in.

short_version.description: Assumes E7 has correct software and is fully configured (Room ID, PAN ID, RF Channel, Onity registries) and OnPortal is set up to pair.

steps.action: Enter Service Parameter Mode on the E7: Press and hold F/C, press and hold MODE, press FAN, then release all.
Use UP arrow to go to the Adr parameter. Press MODE to view value. Set displayed value = 000. Press MODE.

possible_outcomes.meaning: E7 not configured to support Onity Serene locks (Registry 193:9:1 IEEE OUI #0 not set to 00:17:55).
possible_outcomes.resolution: Configure the E7 to support Onity per 'Available Methods to Configure the E7 to support Onity' section, then repeat from Step 1.

possible_outcomes.meaning: E7 is correctly configured and waiting for an advertisement from the lock.
possible_outcomes.resolution: Proceed to Step 3. Process times out after 2 minutes if not completed.

steps.action: On OnPortal, click ENROLL to open the Thermostat Bluetooth Pair screen.
notes: If Inncom server and Onity OnPortal are connected and E7 is online via its Edge Router/B576 floor bridge, check 'ENROLL TO ONPORTAL' so OnPortal sends a CELS mailbox to the lock through the Inncom network as the final pairing step.
If Inncom server/network is not installed/configured yet, do NOT select ENROLL TO ONPORTAL.

steps.action: On OnPortal, click the ENABLE button to start the enrollment process.

expected_e7_sequence.meaning: E7 displays passcode in two 3-digit blocks (e.g., 185918). Repeats for 30 seconds.

failure_case.condition: Passcode not entered into OnPortal within 30 seconds
failure_case.resolution: Return to Step 1 and restart pairing.

steps.action: Enter the 6-digit passcode into the OnPortal passkey field and click ENROLL IN NETWORK.

expected_e7_sequence.meaning: Bonding success - keys and lock ID stored in E7 SNV.
Link terminated - BLE connection for pairing dropped (expected).

results_if_enroll_to_onportal_unchecked.note: E7 and lock ARE paired at this point (BLE icon shows on E7, Ping/Door test will work), but lock remains Offline in OnPortal until Send Status is run separately (see step 6a).

results_if_enroll_to_onportal_checked.sequence: OnPortal attempts 'Send Status' - sends CELS mailbox to lock through Inncom network and waits for reply.

steps.step: 6a (optional, if ENROLL TO ONPORTAL was not selected)
steps.action: To bring a previously-paired lock online later once Inncom network is installed/online: On Maintenance tab, select Portable Programmer > Load Portable Programmer > Bluetooth Scan > select lock > SELECT DEVICE > ENROLL > click SEND STATUS (not Enroll In Network again).
steps.expected_result: 'Successful' window if OnPortal can send/receive a CELS mailbox to the lock through Inncom's network.

steps.action: With E7 and Onity lock paired, verify the pairing.

verification_methods.method: Ping the lock from E7 Pn6 (Ping) service menu
steps: Enter Service Parameter Mode
Go to Pn6 menu item, press MODE (000/Value appears)
Set value to 71
Press FAN to start pinging (~every 8-10 sec)
E7 beeps and increments count on each successful reply (may take 10-15 sec for first reply)
Enter Service Parameter Mode
Go to dor parameter, press MODE (000/Value appears; set to 000 if not already)
Open and close the door
E7 beeps once per opening and displays door open count, continues beeping while open
E7 stops beeping when door is closed/latched

verification_methods.condition: Only if Inncom server and Onity server are connected

long_version.description: Full procedure including OnPortal-side steps, used when Inncom is asked to perform binding/pairing directly (Onity personnel normally do this on-site).
long_version.assumption: Onity has already installed, initialized, and configured the lock with its room number.

checklist: E7 loaded with E7_Bundle_Shelf_Field_v1.2.48.bin or E7_Bundle_Spectre_Field_v1.2.48 or later and powered.
E7 configured as an Onity Lock Proxy (via INNTool/EngINN project, I/O Map 64, or dialog commands).
E7 assigned its required Room ID, PAN ID, RF Channel.

steps.action: On the Onity OnPortal server, initiate device selection.
substeps: Verify PC running OnPortal has a working BLE GigaDongle; start/login to OnPortal.
On Portable Programmer screen, select 'USE BLUETOOTH' (vs PPCOM cable), click BLUETOOTH SCAN.
Select the desired lock from the list, click SELECT DEVICE (lock beeps, shows pink bar, flashes blue ring).
Click ENROLL to open the Thermostat Bluetooth Pair screen.

error_case.symptom: Empty screen instead of Thermostat Bluetooth Pair screen ('SELECT AN APPLICABLE DEVICE TO ENROLL')
error_case.cause: Lock was not initialized, or not initialized as an 'Edge Device'.
error_case.resolution: Re-initialize the lock using Maintenance > Portable Programmer > INITIALIZE, selecting 'INITIALIZE AS EDGE DEVICE', then repeat from Step 2.

decision_point.question: Enroll to OnPortal now or later?
decision_point.option_a: Inncom server/network not installed yet, or E7 not online yet, or don't want to send CELS mailbox now: leave ENROLL TO ONPORTAL unchecked.
decision_point.option_b: Inncom server/network installed and E7 online: check ENROLL TO ONPORTAL to have OnPortal confirm 2-way CELS mailbox communication as the final pairing step.

steps.action: On the E7, place it into pairing mode.
substeps: Enter Service Parameter Mode (hold F/C, hold MODE, press FAN, release all).
UP arrow to Adr parameter, press MODE to view value, set displayed value = 000, press MODE.

possible_outcomes.meaning: Registry 193:9:1 IEEE OUI #0 not set to Onity value 00:17:55.
possible_outcomes.resolution: Configure E7 for Onity support, then repeat from Step 1.

possible_outcomes.meaning: Correctly configured, waiting for lock advertisement (2 min timeout).

steps.action: On OnPortal, click ENABLE to start enrollment/pairing.

expected_e7_sequence.meaning: Displayed in two 3-digit blocks, repeats for 30 seconds.

failure_case.resolution: Return to Step 3 and restart pairing.

steps.action: Enter the passcode into OnPortal, click Enroll In Network.
'Enrolling Device...' then 'Configuring Events' appear on OnPortal.

enroll_to_onportal_unchecked.note: Lock stays Offline in OnPortal (Ping from OnPortal will report 'RMS Server is offline'), but E7-lock BLE pairing is complete: BLE icon shown, Pn6 Ping and door test work.

causes: E7 not actually configured as Onity Proxy (verify Registry 193:9:1 IEEE OUI).
E7 not correctly communicating with TermDMS (check PAN ID/RF Channel/edge router/B576-S, or WAN test).
failure_case.resolution: Fix the underlying issue, then redo enrollment starting at Step 2.

steps.action: If ENROLL TO ONPORTAL was skipped earlier and Inncom is now installed/online: Maintenance > Portable Programmer > Bluetooth Scan > select lock > SELECT DEVICE > ENROLL > click SEND STATUS button (not Enroll In Network).
steps.expected_result: 'Successful' window confirms OnPortal can send a CELS mailbox to the lock and get a reply.

steps.action: With E7 and lock paired, verify with Ping (E7 Pn6 menu) and Door Test, and optionally Ping the lock from OnPortal.
ping_from_e7: Go to Pn6, press MODE, set value to 71
Press FAN to start pinging (~every 8-10 sec, may take 10-15 sec for first reply)
door_test: Go to dor parameter, press MODE, ensure value = 000
Open/close door using handle (must fully latch shut - pushing the switch plunger alone is not sufficient)
E7 beeps once per open event and displays open count; stops beeping when door closes

e7_indication_codes.23: Discovery started (E7 scanning address channels for lock advertisements).
e7_indication_codes.180: Discovery failed (long beep) - E7 never found the lock. Restart pairing: F/C to return to Adr param, set to 000 again, press MODE.
e7_indication_codes.181: Discovery success - lock found, pairing process starting.
e7_indication_codes.six_digit_passcode: Displayed in two 3-digit blocks; enter into OnPortal Enroll screen within 30 seconds.
e7_indication_codes.240: Pairing failed (long beep) - wrong passcode entered, or E7 already paired with this lock. Followed by 25.
e7_indication_codes.220: Bonding failed (long beep) - E7 could not obtain encryption keys/device info from lock. Retry pairing.
e7_indication_codes.221: Bonding success - keys and lock ID stored in E7 SNV (simple non-volatile flash).
e7_indication_codes.25: Link terminated - BLE connection for the pairing process dropped (expected/normal at end of successful pairing).
e7_indication_codes.245: E7 not configured for Onity (Registry 193:9:1 IEEE OUI not set to 00:17:55).

post_pairing_notes: E7 and lock remain paired through E7 resets, factory reboots, or power cycles. Only Onity re-initializing the lock, Inncom replacing the E7, or pairing the E7 with a different lock will un-pair them.
If the Room ID of the E7 must be changed AFTER pairing, Onity must re-initialize the lock to the new Room ID on OnPortal, and the E7 and lock must be re-paired/re-bonded.

BLE icon on E7 display (Registry 9:21:0:2 bit 6 BLE, value 0x7B) indicates E7 and lock are bound and communicating; turns off if no beacon seen from lock for >1 minute, back on automatically when beacons resume.

If E7 has not seen a beacon from the lock for >2 minutes (and SAC alarm feature enabled via Registry 9:13:0:11 bit 0), E7 sends SAC Alarm 0200003C payload 'DEAD'. After 10 minutes with no beacon, E7 sends another 0200003C 'DEAD' alarm and soft-resets to attempt reconnection.

related_dialog_commands.00194: Query E7 BLE pairing status with lock. Reply 0x80 (128) = paired; 0x00 = not paired. Requires E7 Shelf 1.2.48 / Spectre 3.2.48 or later.
related_dialog_commands.0019100050040: Activates I/O Map 64 on E7 to configure required registries (9:13 CELS, 9:21 DISPLAY, 193:9:1 IEEE OUI) for Onity support. Does NOT perform pairing - only configuration.
related_dialog_commands.0018CC1090100: Read Registry 193:9:1 IEEE OUI #0 - should reply 0017550000000047 or 0017550000000040 to confirm Onity proxy configuration.
